infobox.Guest stars:: Deon Richmond Billy Zane Val Kilmer Floriana Lima Madison Smith Robert Underwood Brian Barry

sections.heading: Intro
sections.text: "The Break-Up" is the tenth and final episode of Season Eight and the series, overall. Shawn decides to move to San Francisco to be with Juliet , but struggles to find a way to break the news to Gus .

sections.heading: Contents
sections.text: 1 Plot Summary 2 Trivia 3 Quotes 4 Digital Releases

sections.heading: Plot Summary
sections.text:
Gus is showing Shawn his amazing new office at Pacific Nutraceutical Products . There's a fully stocked kitchen, Gus gets a luxury company car, three weeks vacation a year, and a slew of sweet perks. Shawn is impressed and Gus is thrilled to finally have a job where his work is appreciated and rewarded. In addition to all that good stuff, Gus is happy to report he thinks his co-worker ( Floriana Lima ), a beautiful woman whose middle name is literally "Love", could be the perfect woman for him. Gus hasn't made his move yet, but is hopeful he will soon.
The flashback ends for a beat, and we cut back to the Psych office , where Shawn is speaking into the camera he had set up. He explains that after seeing how happy Gus was with his new life, he knew that it was his time to focus on his own happiness; he knew that it was time to close Psych and to leave Santa Barbara to be with Juliet .
We flash back to Shawn telling this news to Juliet over the phone. She is elated that they will finally be together in the same city, but wonders how Gus has taken the news. Shawn says he hasn't told him yet.
Shawn and Gus arrive at the station. Shawn is doing everything he can to avoid breaking the news to Gus that he's moving away and closing Psych for good. But, he knows he has to do it soon, so he musters up enough courage to tell Gus that he has bad news to report. Before Shawn can explain to Gus what the news is, Lassiter interrupts and says they need help on a murder case. On their way to the crime scene, Head Detective Betsy Brannigan explains to Lassiter that she hasn't hired a new Junior Detective yet because she prefers to work alone. Gus advises her to not work alone because working alone is the saddest of situations to be in. Shawn overhears Gus saying this and begins to feel even more guilty for his decision to leave Gus behind.
They arrive at the crime scene and find the body of Warren Dern ( Robert Underwood ) lying dead on his living room floor. Gus is trying to get Shawn to tell him what the bad news is but Shawn, again, avoids telling him. Betsy explains that Warren had no next of kin and that his in-case-of-emergency contact is his best friend and business partner Ian Collins ( Billy Zane ), who is there at the crime scene with them answering questions. Ian explains he and Warren were in real estate together until Warren left the partnership to start his own venture, which Ian found odd because the business they ran together was doing well. While Shawn is listening to all this, he can't help but notice that Ian and Warren's situation parallels his situation with Gus. He becomes visibly and vocally uncomfortable.
In that moment, Lassiter realizes why Shawn is acting like this, so he takes Shawn aside to have a talk. He tells Shawn he knows he's leaving Santa Barbara to be with Juliet and that he hasn't told Gus yet. Lassiter advises him to take Gus to a happy place filled with old memories, so that when he breaks the news to Gus, the blow will be a bit softened. Shawn thinks this is a great idea, so he hugs Lassiter and tells him this is the best advice he's ever given him.
At the morgue, Betsy and Woody discuss the scratches on the body, the dirt all over him and the raccoon scat found under his fingernails. Betsy thinks he was rushing to uncover something buried in the ground. Woody adds that there were also traces of moss and a rare breed of mushroom. Armed with these clues, Betsy thinks she knows where Warren was digging before he was killed. She figured it out before Shawn did, which worries Gus. Their jobs at the SBPD are starting to become obsolete because Betsy is always two steps ahead of them.
They arrive at the hole Warren dug up in the woods. Betsy finds it odd that the hole is so small. Shawn immediately starts looking for clues, but every clue Shawn sees, Brannigan sees, too. She spots two different sets of footprints near the hole; a size 10 and a half shoe, belonging to Warren, and the other, a size 12 shoe, possibly belonging to an unknown accomplice. She finds a tollbooth with a camera nearby and thinks it could be her ticket to identifying the killer.
She brings these clues back to the SBPD to do some further investigating. She watches the tollbooth footage and confirms that Warren had indeed driven to the hole with another person, perhaps an unknown co-conspirator. She discovers this must be Klaus Goedecke ( Brian Barry ) based on ATM footage, and quickly finds a location on him. They head to the hotel where Goedecke is staying, enter his room, and find him lying dead on the floor.
Back at PNP, Shawn tells Love that Gus is going to need someone to take care of him. However, Love doesn't even know who Gus is and now Shawn is even more worried knowing the girl of Gus's dreams may never be his. The bright future Shawn saw happening for Gus is slowly starting to dim.
Shawn hides when Gus comes in talking to his boss Mr. Richmond ( Deon Richmond ). Gus tells Mr. Richmond about his plans to own a house and maybe have children someday, and Shawn is shocked he never knew any of that.
Shawn heads to Henry's house for some advice. While Henry is busy prepping to fill in for a professor who teaches a criminology class, Shawn says he needs to tell someone something serious, but he doesn't know how. Henry gives him a bit of general advice. He tells Shawn to be straightforward, but to break the news to this person while he or she is in a good frame of mind; that way the bad news won't feel as devastating. This advice, coupled with Lassiter's advice, helps Shawn determine what he needs to do. He needs to solve the case with Gus before Betsy solves it. When they do solve it, they'll be elated, and the news of Shawn leaving Gus won't feel as bad.
Shawn brings Gus back to Goedecke's hotel room to find more clues. Gus thinks it's a lost cause because Betsy is probably very close to solving the case already. However, Shawn ends up finding new clues that lead them closer to the truth. It turns out Goedecke was actually a hitman. His hotel room wasn't robbed or broken into; Goedecke let the killer into his room because he knew him. Shawn then finds a hidden map leading to a buried treasure in the woods. Gus says they already found the hole in the woods earlier, but after he takes a closer look at the map, he realizes that the hole they found earlier was in a different location. They were digging in the wrong place. Whoever killed Goedecke was looking for this map, but couldn't find it.
Shawn and Gus follow the map to the correct location of the buried treasure in the woods. Gus starts digging and is mortified to find a body buried there. They bring it back to the morgue and Woody tells Shawn the body belongs to Chris Cheramie . He was killed with a 9 mm gun, and there are bullets riddled all over his body. From this, they know Goedecke wasn't the one who killed him because a professional hitman would have been more accurate. Gus does an Internet search and finds out that Chris was a land speculator who came to town to bid on a lot, but he never made it to the auction. The people that actually won the auction were Warren Dern and his partner Ian Collins. From this, they determine the real killer is Collins. Woody points out that he found brake dust on Goedecke's clothes. Shawn then asks Woody if his car was impounded, and it turns out it was.
Shawn and Gus go to check out the car in the impound lot. Underneath the car, they find a 9mm gun in a plastic bag. Gus suggests they get this evidence over to Lassiter immediately, but Shawn tells him they need to make a stop first. Shawn takes Gus to their old high school, Bosseigh High . Gus is confused as to why they are there and tells Shawn that he's been acting crazy this entire investigation. Shawn finally admits to Gus that this is their last case together, and that he wanted to take Gus to their old high school because it's filled with great memories. Gus is confused. Before Shawn can explain, Ian Collins shows up pointing a gun at the both of them.
It turns out Ian had been following them the whole day. Ian knows they have the murder weapon and threatens them at gunpoint, telling them to hand it over. Shawn tells Ian he knows what really happened and starts to break down the case. Three years ago, Ian and Warren killed Chris Cheramie to win the auction. Ian Collins buried the body while Warren buried the murder weapon. When Warren abruptly left his and Ian's business, he wanted to use the gun as leverage against Ian, who hired Goedecke to kill Warren. When Goedecke went to kill Warren, Warren told him that he had the murder weapon with Ian's fingerprints on it. After retrieving the murder weapon from Warren, Goedecke killed Warren with his own gun and fled the scene.
Collins knows he's been caught. He chases Shawn and Gus through campus, gun in hand. Shawn and Gus make a quick getaway in the school's learner driver car , which has two steering wheels, two gas pedals, and two brake pedals. Ian drives after them, trying to catch up. In a panic, Shawn texts Henry for back up. Henry is in the middle of teaching the criminology class, so he tells the few students who actually showed up that they're going on a field trip.
Henry and his students arrive at the road Shawn and Gus are being chased down. Henry spots Ian's car and shoots a tire out, bringing him to a halt. The cops show up and Henry guides Collins over to be arrested. The jaded online students are impressed, saying "Best Class Ever!"
The case is now solved, and Shawn knows the time has come to finally break the news to Gus, but he can't. He can't bring himself to do it in person. We then learn that the video of Shawn recounting this whole story is actually a "goodbye DVD" he made for Gus explaining why he couldn't tell him this news in person.
When Gus watches the DVD, he's shocked. He listens to Shawn's whole story about why he's leaving Santa Barbara and why it was so hard to tell him. In the video, Shawn says to Gus that he knows he'll be OK without him, and that he believes he was the one holding Gus back; that he had kept him from the life that he really wanted, the life he truly deserved. If he hadn't barged into Gus's office eight years ago and said, "We’re going to play detective whether you like it or not," maybe Gus would have had a different life; perhaps a better life. Gus turns the video off and tries to process it all.
Moments later, Gus heads over to the Psych office to see if Shawn is still there. Gus is surprised to find Henry there, as well. Henry is there scoping out the office with a measuring tape, appearing as though he may use the space for himself someday. Henry tells Gus that Shawn made him a goodbye DVD too. It turns out Shawn sent goodbye DVDs to everyone; to Woody, to Lassiter and even to the mysterious Officer Dobson ( Val Kilmer ), whose face we finally see after years of solely mentioning his name.
In the goodbye DVD to Lassiter, Shawn recorded himself admitting the truth that he's not a real psychic. Lassiter watches it, but shuts it off right before the part where Shawn's about to admit it. Lassiter knows what Shawn was about to say, and he didn't feel the need to hear it. Shawn did more good for the SBPD than Lassiter could have ever imagined, and for that he will be forever grateful, even though he'd never admit that out loud. Just then, Betsy stops by Lassiter's office to tell him she wants McNab to be her Junior Detective.
Although Buzz had the lowest score on the detective's exam, Betsy isn't fazed. She believes that, under her tutelage, she can mold Buzz into a brilliant detective. Lassiter is shocked to hear this, but trusts Betsy's instincts and approves it anyway. Buzz steps in wearing a suit and tie, excited about his new position on the force.
Shawn arrives in San Francisco and immediately goes to see Juliet, who's in the middle of investigating a crime scene. Chief Vick is there, too. Suddenly, to everyone's surprise, Gus shows up. He's incredibly angry that Shawn left without saying goodbye. He then launches into the story detailing the reason why he drove all the way to San Francisco to tell him that in person... Gus was about to give a presentation at work, but found Shawn's goodbye DVD on his desk and decided to watch it first. Shawn's words made Gus realize that his "real job" as a pharmaceutical salesman was never his real passion and that he doesn't want to live his life behind a desk anymore. He admits that what he said to Mr. Richmond was not true and he was just playing the part. Solving crimes with Shawn has been the best part of his life for the last eight years, and he doesn't want to give that up. So, he decided to do something really bold, the boldest thing he's ever done, and move to San Francisco to keep Psych and their friendship alive.
Shawn is shocked and overjoyed to hear this news. They're going to set up Psych in San Francisco and solve crimes for Chief Vick and the SFPD full-time, and it's going to be awesome. Juliet and the Chief say they already have a consultant, but Shawn isn't worried. He knows he and Gus will do a better job.
Shawn's next move is to tell Juliet that he's never going to shy away from the big decisions ever again. He launches into a lengthy speech, telling Juliet that when he's with her he feels fearless and unbreakable, and that he never wants to be without her. He pulls out an engagement ring and proposes to Juliet.
Juliet says yes, but before Shawn can put the ring on her finger, a mugger steals it from Shawn's hands and gets away. Shawn, Gus, and Juliet immediately hop in the Driver's Ed car. They chase after the robber, and it feels just like old times. An exciting new chapter in each of their lives has officially begun.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: Chief Vick's mention of a consultant who is "alphabetizing the pantry" is a clear reference to the obsessive-compulsive San Francisco "police consultant" Adrian Monk from Monk , another USA Network series. USA Network promos from 2006 had previously shown the characters of Shawn and Monk together. As he says goodbye to Gus in the break room, Mr. Richmond affectionately says "Take it easy, bud." This is a reference to the common misconception among Psych characters that Gus played "Bud" on The Cosby Show , a joke doubly meaningful in this instance as it is actor Deon Richmond who plays "Mr. Richmond" who actually played "Bud". Floriana Lima , who portrays Love , appears on A Million Little Things as Darcy, a love interest of James Roday Rodriguez 's Gary. Henry asking Maximillian , "How many hats are in the room?" is a direct call-back to the opening scene of the pilot , where Henry asks Shawn the exact same question. Henry's examination of the Psych office is explained in Psych 2: Lassie Come Home - - he's subletting it to Maisie 's Le Litière and mentions that several other companies have also made use of the space. Gus tells Shawn that children terrify him but that he's sure they won't at some point. In Psych 3: This Is Gus , he and Selene have their first child together. Billy Zane and Val Kilmer are mentioned throughout the show as two of Shawn's heroes and both finally appear in this episode, Zane as Ian Collins and Kilmer (in a non-speaking cameo) as Dobson . Zane was almost cast three times before this episode. Ian and Warren's murder victim Chris Cheramie is named after Psych producer Chris Cheramie , and the Gordon Mark hotel is named after producer Gordon Mark . Show creator Steve Franks said this script was more difficult to write than the musical . Shawn's "Stay gold, Ponywood" is a reference to "Stay gold, Ponyboy" from S.E. Hinton 's novel The Outsiders , a quote referenced again by Pony in a Onesie in Psych: The Movie . The mention that "they're digging in the wrong place" is a nod to Raiders of the Lost Ark . Also, during Henry's Criminology class, the map and words written on the blackboard behind him are the same things that were written on Indiana Jones' blackboard in Raiders . Shawn's destruction of the wall of snacks didn't go as planned, but James Roday Rodriguez walked through the shelving unit anyway. The number of soda fountains at PNP was doubled because it didn't seem like enough. The big car chase was shot over two days. The scenes in the woods were shot on the same road as the mummy crash in S2E16 " Shawn (and Gus) of the Dead ". There was also a scene shot at the planetarium where S1E10 " From the Earth to the Starbucks " is set, but it was cut from the final show. A bat swooped through the set during the hotel raid. The pool of blood Shawn stands in is actually made of rubber as the crew wasn't allowed to disturb the floor. The episode was actually shot third to last. The song playing as the final scene fades to black is "We Close Our Eyes" by Oingo Boingo.

sections.heading: Quotes
sections.text:
Henry: I got roped into filling in for a criminology course at the college for a stupid ex-partner who went and let his liver fail. Shawn: Come on, dad, you've never been afraid of anything in your whole life. Henry: Yeah, I'm just unsure of the rules. Let me ask you, how much are you allowed to scream, huh? Shawn: Why don't you go with medium loud, and keep your belly button covered no matter what? Henry: Sound advice. I need to go. Gotta buy bullets.
Ian: I have been following you two all day. Who finds a murder weapon and goes to a Wendy's drive-through? Shawn: Hungry people. People who appreciate value and quality and don't have a lot of time. Gus: Besides, we had Mexican yesterday, so Del Taco was out. Ian: You went to Del Taco. Gus: For dessert .
Shawn: [in his goodbye video to Woody] Woodrow, I'll miss you most of all. Stay gold, Ponywood. Stay gold. Woody: [chuckling] You know I will, Shawn. [Shawn smiles before the video ends and the screen goes black] Woody: [confused] Shawn? Shawn? This is the weirdest Skype call I've ever been on.
